title: Climate Change Impact Intelligence System
description: A national-scale environmental monitoring platform that consolidates climate data, environmental risk indicators, and emissions tracking.

body.raw:
## Project Overview

The Climate Change Impact Intelligence System is a national-scale environmental monitoring platform that consolidates climate data, environmental risk indicators, emissions tracking, and sustainability progress into a single, continuously updated intelligence dashboard.

It serves as decision infrastructure for climate adaptation and mitigation, tracking exposure to environmental risk, emissions trends, and national climate commitments.

## Core Features

- **National Climate Risk Monitor**: Real-time overview of temperature anomalies, rainfall deviations, and drought severity across the country.
- **Emissions & Carbon Tracking**: Monitoring GHG profiles against national commitments (NDCs) and tracking deforestation via satellite alerts.
- **Environmental Health Indicators**: Tracking air quality, water body health, and land degradation indices.
- **Agricultural Climate Impact Tracker**: Connecting climate conditions to food security with seasonal crop stress indices and yield forecast deviations.
- **Sustainability Progress Tracker**: Monitoring progress against the Paris Agreement, SDG 13, and national environmental policies.
- **Climate Projection Explorer**: Forward-looking tool for 20, 30, and 50-year climate projections by region.

## Impact

The system makes Ghana's international climate commitments monitorable and accountability enforceable, while providing the analytical foundation to access global climate finance.

title: COVID-19 Dashboard
description: A real-time public health intelligence platform built to give Ghanaians a clear, continuously updated picture of the pandemic's trajectory.

body.raw:
## Project Overview

The COVID-19 Dashboard is a real-time public health intelligence platform built to give Ghanaians — citizens, healthcare workers, government officials, and institutions — a clear, continuously updated picture of the pandemic's trajectory across the country.

Where official health communications have historically relied on press briefings, PDF reports, and static weekly summaries, this platform makes pandemic data live, geographic, and interactive.

## Core Features

- **National Situation Summary**: A single-screen headline view displaying metrics like total confirmed cases, new cases, deaths, and vaccination progress, updated daily.
- **Regional Outbreak Map**: Interactive choropleth map of Ghana's 16 regions, color-coded by outbreak intensity with district-level drill-downs.
- **Vaccination Progress Tracker**: Granular tracking of first, second, and booster dose coverage against national targets.
- **Healthcare System Capacity Monitor**: Real-time visibility into ICU bed occupancy, ventilator availability, and treatment center capacity.
- **Epidemiological Trend Analysis**: Deep analytical layer for professionals, including Rt estimates and epidemic curve wavy wave annotations.
- **Variant Surveillance Panel**: Tracking genomic sequencing results to monitor variant dominance and emergence.

## Impact

The platform closes the information asymmetry gap, enabling faster decision-making, better resource allocation (ICU beds, etc.), and building public trust through transparency.

title: National Budget Transparency Portal
description: An interactive platform that transforms Ghana's national budget into a clear, searchable, and visually accessible resource for public engagement.

body.raw:
## Project Overview

The National Budget Transparency Portal is a public-facing platform that transforms Ghana's national budget from a dense government document into a clear, searchable, and visually accessible resource for citizens, journalists, and researchers.

It ingests official budget data — allocations, expenditure releases, and actual spending — to make fiscal transparency a practical reality.

## Core Features

- **Budget Overview Dashboard**: High-level summary of revenue, expenditure, and deficit/surplus positions at a glance.
- **Sector Allocation Explorer**: Deep-dives into Education, Health, Agriculture, and Infrastructure funding with international benchmarking.
- **Ministry & Agency Explorer**: Searchable directory of budget profiles and execution rates for all spending entities.
- **Geographic Budget Map**: Mapping sub-national fiscal data, including regional development funds and infrastructure project locations.
- **Expenditure Tracker**: Tracking actual spending versus allocations to identify under-execution and oversight gaps.
- **Revenue Tracker**: Monitoring tax collection performance and trends against national targets.
- **Public Debt Monitor**: Making Ghana's domestic and external debt position accessible and understandable.

## Impact

By making budget data accessible, the portal improves oversight, reduces accountability deficits, and empowers informed advocacy and journalism in public finance management.

title: SDG Performance Tracker
description: A comprehensive platform for monitoring Ghana's progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals with granular development intelligence.

body.raw:
## Project Overview

The SDG Performance Tracker is a development monitoring platform that measures Ghana's progress across the UN Sustainable Development Goals — tracking 232 indicators and translating them into strategic intelligence.

It serves both for external accountability with international partners and internal decision-making to guide sector investments.

## Core Features

- **Goal-Level Deep Dives**: Dedicated analytical sections for all 17 goals, showing current values, baselines, and 2030 targets.
- **Geographic Disaggregation**: Regional SDG maps to identify disparities and ensure "leaving no one behind" is measurable.
- **Equity Analysis**: Disaggregating progress by sex, age, wealth quintile, and urban/rural location.
- **Investment Gap Analysis**: Connecting indicator performance to investment needs and identifying private vs. public sector potential.
- **VNR Support Module**: Automating the data backbone for Ghana's Voluntary National Reviews to the United Nations.
- **Data Quality Monitor**: Transparency about data methodology, reference years, and next update dates.

## Impact

The tracker acts as a national learning system, helping Ghana identify what works in its development agenda and positioning the country to access development finance through demonstrated impact.

title: Sovereign Debt & Fiscal Risk Platform
description: A specialized data platform for managing national debt sustainability, fiscal risk projections, and economic governance.

body.raw:
## Project Overview

The Sovereign Debt & Fiscal Risk Intelligence Platform is a financial reporting and analytics system that visualizes Ghana's public debt portfolio, economic indicators, and fiscal risk projections.

It is designed for real-time risk management by the Ministry of Finance and for enhanced parliamentary and public oversight of the country's fiscal future.

## Core Features

- **National Debt Overview**: Clear summary of total public debt (domestic and external) as absolute figures and % of GDP.
- **Debt Portfolio Explorer**: Detailed breakdown by creditor class (Bilateral, Multilateral, Commercial), currency, and maturity.
- **Debt Service Profile**: Visualization of future debt service obligations for the next 20 years, identifying peak service years.
- **Fiscal Performance Monitor**: Tracking revenue performance, expenditure execution, and primary balance trends.
- **Debt Sustainability Analysis (DSA)**: Accessible dashboards showing risk classification and stress test scenarios (growth shocks, exchange rate depreciation).
- **Economic Context**: Real-time tracking of GDP growth, inflation, exchange rates, and foreign exchange reserves.
- **IMF Program Tracker**: Monitoring milestones, benchmarks, and disbursement schedules under the ECF program.

## Impact

The platform builds fiscal credibility through transparency, providing the warning signs needed to prevent debt crises and supporting Ghana's return to international capital markets.
